gap_summaries.title: Working-tree CRLF line endings on changed files
gap_summaries.detail: Reviewer reproduction with `git stash` + `git checkout` on a `core.autocrlf=true` Windows checkout caused the working tree to contain CRLF for both changed files while the git index stayed LF (verified via `git ls-files --eol`). The `test_lf_line_endings` discipline gate (m07a) reads raw bytes and failed under that workspace state.
gap_summaries.resolution: Rewrote both files' bytes from CRLF -> LF in the working tree. Index was already LF, so no commit was required; `git diff` for the two source files is empty after the fix. Test re-run is green, ruff check is clean, ruff format reports zero diffs, coverage on failure_triage.py is 100% (threshold 85).

production_notes: Artifact is a pure-functional library (no HTTP routes, no argparse, no I/O, no concurrency, no shared state). Production-readiness profile collapses to library-level discipline, which is fully satisfied: no secrets, no external calls, typed inputs from M01 dataclasses, REQ-06 no-raise contract honoured via getattr-with-default for spec-named-but-M01-absent fields, REQ-12 exception-propagation contract honoured by the thin generator, no TODO/FIXME/HACK (test-enforced).

spec_requirements_status.REQ-06: covered - classify(event) -> Classification, pure, no raise on M01 events
spec_requirements_status.REQ-08: covered - 6 substring rules in declaration order, error_class + error_kind both inspected
spec_requirements_status.REQ-09: covered - CRASH/HIGH default, exit_signal SIGPIPE/SIGHUP/network -> NETWORK_ERROR implied
spec_requirements_status.REQ-10: covered - GATE_DEFER/HIGH default, plateau substring or attempt_count>3 -> REPEATED_RETRY+PLATEAU
spec_requirements_status.REQ-11: covered - REVIEW_REJECTED/MEDIUM default, trigger startswith 'policy:' -> POLICY_VIOLATION+REVIEW_REJECTED
spec_requirements_status.REQ-12: covered - classify_stream generator, lazy, propagates iterator exceptions verbatim
spec_requirements_status.REQ-13: covered - imports limited to enum/dataclasses/collections.abc + core.telemetry_events; allowlist test green
spec_requirements_status.REQ-14: covered - 13 behavioural matrix tests (one per FailureClass) + mixed-event stream round-trip
spec_requirements_status.REQ-15: covered - every behavioural test asserts on primary + implies membership + confidence; no file I/O in behavioural tests; aggregate runtime 31ms (budget 2s)

quality_gates_status.lf_line_endings: pass (after working-tree fix; index was already LF)
quality_gates_status.project_wide_regression: pass (no new failures vs baseline; pre-existing 14 fails / 31 errors unrelated to failure_triage)

verification_commands_run: python -m ruff check <files> -> All checks passed!
python -m ruff format --check <files> -> 2 files already formatted
PYTHONPATH=skills/bmad-story-automator/src python -m coverage run --include=...failure_triage.py -m unittest tests.test_failure_triage -> OK, 100% coverage
